---
title: "Group Analysis Scripts"
subtitle: "Working code from workshop groups"
---

Each group has a subfolder for their analysis scripts. Scripts will appear here as groups push their work during the workshop.

| Group | Question | Folder |
|-------|----------|--------|
| Group A | Key epidemiological parameters | `analysis/group-A/` |
| Group B | Serial interval distribution | `analysis/group-B/` |
| Group C | Reporting delays and underreporting | `analysis/group-C/` |
| Group D | Vaccine effectiveness | `analysis/group-D/` |

---

## Contributing Your Scripts

### First time setup

You will need a [GitHub account](https://github.com) and [Git](https://git-scm.com/downloads) installed. Do this before the workshop starts.

Clone the repository:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/kylieainslie/lorentz_center_workshop.git
cd lorentz_center_workshop
```

Switch to your group's branch — replace `A` with your group letter (`A`, `B`, `C`, or `D`):

```bash
git checkout group-A
```

The four branches (`group-A`, `group-B`, `group-C`, `group-D`) already exist on GitHub. Do not create a new branch.

### Adding your scripts

Put your scripts in your group's folder, e.g. `analysis/group-A/`. Use numbered filenames to indicate order:

```
analysis/group-A/
├── 01_explore_data.R
├── 02_analysis_plan.md
└── 03_main_analysis.R
```

### Saving and sharing your work

After adding or editing scripts, commit and push to your group's branch:

```bash
git add analysis/group-A/
git commit -m "Add initial data exploration"
git push origin group-A
```

Push regularly so your group members always have the latest version.

### Pulling updates from teammates

If a teammate has pushed changes, pull them before you start working:

```bash
git pull origin group-A
```

### Returning to the workshop repo on subsequent days

```bash
git checkout group-A
git pull origin group-A
```
